Title: KENNEDY SPACE CENTER, FLA. -  NASA astronaut Steven W. Lindsey (left) listens to a briefing about the pressurized module of the Japanese Experiment Module (JEM) in the Space Station Processing Facility.  This major component of JEM, named "Kibo" (Hope), is Japan's primary contribution to the International Space Station and arrived at KSC on June 4.  Lindsey is assigned to command STS-119, an upcoming Space Shuttle flight to the Station.
CreditLine: NASA Image and Video Library (public domain)
